recentFailures.suggestedFix: Use await expect(locator).toHaveText() instead of synchronous assertion

errorContext.testId: [FAIL] Cart count mismatch after adding 2 products
errorContext.failedStep: Then the cart badge should show 3 items

errorContext.testId: [FAIL] Locked out user should reach inventory page
errorContext.failedStep: Then the user should be on the inventory page

rca.description: Cart item count badge is not being updated after addToCart(). A synchronous assertion is reading a stale DOM value before React/the app has re-rendered the badge counter.
affectedTests: [FAIL] Cart count mismatch after adding 2 products
rca.evidence: Consistent Expected: 3 / Received: 2 across both runs — deterministic, not flaky. Difference is always exactly 1.

rca.description: The locked_out_user credential is intentionally blocked by SauceDemo. The test incorrectly asserts the user reaches /inventory.html, but the designed behavior is to remain on the login page with an error banner.
affectedTests: [FAIL] Locked out user should reach inventory page
rca.evidence: URL always stays at '/' (login page) — SauceDemo's intended behavior for locked_out_user, not a transient environment issue.

affectedTests: [FAIL] Cart count mismatch after adding 2 products
failureMapping.description: Cart badge counter fails to reflect the correct item count after addToCart(). This directly impacts the checkout flow validation and could indicate a real regression in cart state management.

affectedTests: [FAIL] Locked out user should reach inventory page
failureMapping.description: Test expectation is misaligned with the application's designed behavior for locked_out_user. SauceDemo intentionally blocks this user — the test must assert on the error state, not a success path.

codeFixes.testId: [FAIL] Cart count mismatch after adding 2 products
codeFixes.issue: Synchronous assertion on cart badge — reads stale DOM value before update
codeFixes.explanation: Playwright's built-in await expect(...).toHaveText() automatically retries the assertion until the DOM updates or times out. A plain expect() with await textContent() grabs the value at a single instant — before the badge has updated — causing the count mismatch.

codeFixes.testId: [FAIL] Locked out user should reach inventory page
codeFixes.issue: Test asserts a URL that SauceDemo will never serve to a locked-out user
codeFixes.explanation: SauceDemo intentionally redirects locked_out_user back to the login page with an error banner. The fix aligns the assertion with the application's designed behavior: verify the error message is shown rather than expecting a success navigation.

suggestedImprovements.title: Replace all synchronous assertions with Playwright auto-retry expects
suggestedImprovements.description: Audit the full suite for any expect(await element.textContent()) patterns. Replace with await expect(locator).toHaveText() / .toHaveURL() / .toBeVisible() to eliminate timing-related failures.

suggestedImprovements.title: Align negative-path test expectations with app's designed error states
suggestedImprovements.description: Review all tests using locked_out_user, error_user, and invalid_user. Each should assert on the error banner or error state — not on success-path URLs or elements that will never appear.

suggestedImprovements.title: Add step-level cart state verification
suggestedImprovements.description: Add granular assertions after each individual addToCart() call to isolate which product or step triggers the count mismatch, rather than only asserting the final total.

suggestedImprovements.title: Enable Playwright retries for CI runs
suggestedImprovements.description: Set retries: 2 in playwright.config.ts. This catches genuine flakiness vs consistent defects, and prevents single transient failures from blocking the pipeline.

impactAssessment.businessImpact: The cart count mismatch could indicate a real regression in add-to-cart UX — a user adding 3 items seeing a badge of 2 would lose trust in the cart. The locked-out user failure generates false-negative CI alerts obscuring real failures.
impactAssessment.regressionRisk: Medium — same 2 tests failed identically across 2 consecutive runs (09:14 → 11:44 → 11:53), confirming a consistent deterministic defect rather than a transient environment issue.

actionItems.title: Fix cart badge assertion to use toHaveText()
actionItems.description: Replace synchronous cart count assertion with Playwright's auto-retrying locator assertion in cart.spec.ts

actionItems.title: Fix locked-out user test to assert on error banner
actionItems.description: Replace toHaveURL('/inventory.html') with toBeVisible() + toContainText('locked out') on the SauceDemo error banner element

actionItems.title: Audit all negative-path tests for incorrect success assertions
actionItems.description: Review locked_out_user, error_user and invalid_user tests across the suite — ensure each asserts on designed error behavior

actionItems.title: Enable Playwright retries: 2 in CI configuration
actionItems.description: Add retries: 2 to playwright.config.ts to differentiate flaky from consistently failing tests and improve pipeline stability

actionItems.title: Add per-step cart state assertions
actionItems.description: Add badge count checks after each individual addToCart() call to precisely locate the step where the count diverges from expected
